item.itunes:title: 中華民國修憲有多難！？Amending the Constitution of the Republic of China|#我的學習筆記 #539 ‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪‪@mynotebooks
item.itunes:author: 我的學習筆記
item.itunes:summary:
Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. There has been a lot of debate over constitutional. interpretation lately. In addition to the death penalty,. The five congressional bills also received backlash from other chambers. Entered the constitutional process. But the Constitution, as the fundamental law of the country,. It not only regulates the scope of government power. It also protects the rights of the people. But the law needs to keep pace with the times. There is no law. You can never revise with the times. So many countries. There is a record of constitutional amendments. Even redefining the constitution. So today let’s talk about. The history of constitutional amendment in the Republic of China. Constitution of the Republic of China. The earliest can be traced back to 1912. That is, the wartime constitution of the first year. of the Republic of China. The Provisional Constitution of the Tutelage Period of 1931. The 1936 Draft Constitution. The constitution was finally drafted in 1946. In fact, we have talked about these. Friends who have forgotten can review it again. The Constitution of the Republic of China in 1947. That is, it was promulgated and implemented in 1947. The full text has 14 chapters in total. 175 articles. The main feature is to highlight the Three Principles of the People. The concept of sovereignty of the people. Clearly define the protection of people’s freedom and rights. Central government system that stipulates the separation. of five powers. There is also a system of local self-government. Directly express the central and local. The division of authority adopts the equal power system. And clearly state the basic national policies, etc. But then you also knew. Our national fortune is so bad. War. In 1948. In response to the Civil War. Subsidiary provisions implementing the Constitution. That is the. "Provisional Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion" And because of the mobilization to suppress the rebellion. Temporary Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion. will take precedence over the Constitution of the Republic of China. Until 1991. Former President Lee Teng-hui conducted. After the first amendment to the Constitution. The Temporary Provisions of Mobilization. for the Suppression of Rebellion were abolished. Since then, there have been seven constitutional amendments. During his 12-year presidency, former President Lee. There have been 6 constitutional amendments in total. The most recent one was in 2005. That is, during the presidency of Chen Shui-bian. So have you discovered. We haven't amended the constitution in nearly 20 years. There is nothing wrong with this constitution. Don’t need to keep up with the times? Or is the threshold for constitutional amendment too high? Is it impossible to achieve? Let's start with former President Lee. After the lifting of martial law in 1987. Politics gradually moved towards democratization. Calls for constitutional reform are beginning to emerge. President Chiang Ching-kuo died the following year. Vice President Lee Teng-hui succeeded. During his 12 years in office. Passed 6 constitutional amendments. Complete the bloodless "Quiet Revolution" Let the Republic of China move away from authoritarianism. Transformed into a democratic country. Brother Hui is the first. Directly through the elected president. Known as "Mr. Democracy" In January 1988. Huigo succeeds as president. Two years later. Old delegates to the National Assembly. I feel like Xiao Jiang is gone. No one can cure us. So I want to expand my power.

But I didn't expect it to cause public dissatisfaction. The Wild Lily Student Movement in March. Require the government to truly implement democratic constitutionalism. Congressional re-election. In fact, Brother Hui has been annoyed with this group. of people for a long time. So he followed the surging public sentiment. Brother Hui convened a "national affairs meeting" outside the system. Harnessing the power of civil society for reform. To fight against the conservative forces in the Kuomintang. Join hands with the Democratic Progressive Party. to promote constitutional amendment. So the first constitutional amendment in 1991. It is against this background that. First, the first senior national representative. Responsible for the first phase. of procedural constitutional amendment. First, abolish the Temporary Provisions on Mobilization. for the Suppression of Rebellion. The Republic of China Free Area. That is, Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu are within the scope. Re-election of the Second Central People's Representatives. And re-elect regularly. Implementing the democratic concept of "sovereignty of the people" Let the Wannian National Era go down in history. So that the second national representative. Can be produced legitimately. Next year, the newly elected second national representative. Conducting the second phase of substantive constitutional reform. Legalize local self-government. Provide legal basis for local autonomy. and popular election of heads of state. Also the former National Congress representatives. The power to elect the president was revoked. The presidential term was changed to 4 years. And there's been a lot of noise lately. Supervisory power of the Supervisory Committee. No longer elected. Appointed by the President. But this also makes our Supervisory Commission. Loss of the conditions for a people's representative body. For more details. You can go and have a look. We previously did a study on. "Should the Supervisory Yuan be abolished?" The Republic of China's unique tripartite system. The Legislative Yuan. National Assembly and Control Yuan. Only the Legislative Yuan. and the bi-parliamentary system of the National Assembly. The personnel approval power originally belonged to the Control Yuan. Delegates to the National Assembly. The most important thing is. The Constitutional Court was established. and to hear cases of unconstitutional dissolution of political parties. That is to say, according to the amended provisions. As long as the purpose or behavior of the political party is. Endangering the Republic of China. Or a liberal democratic constitutional order. The Supreme Court has the power. to dissolve the unconstitutional political party. It sounds really fierce. But this constitutional amendment. It also brought about a major change. in the domestic political situation. The Democratic Progressive Party has successively. In the county and city mayoral elections. The choices are getting more and more beautiful. However, there were serious conflicts within the Kuomintang. Some even left the party and set up a "new party" The third constitutional amendment in July 1994. Because the previous constitutional amendment. Only the right of the National Assembly representatives. to vote for the president was abolished. But it didn't say how to choose the president next time. So this constitutional amendment. Direct election of the president. Written into the revised provisions. And limit the scope of the Premier's countersignature power. The constitutional system of our country has also changed. from "Formal Cabinet System" Transition to a semi-presidential system or a dual-head system. It also clearly states that "mountain compatriots" are included in all legal provisions. Corrected to "Aboriginal" Pay more attention to the rights of indigenous peoples. The Legislative Yuan election at the end of 1995.

The Kuomintang won 85 seats. Only 3 more seats than the majority. Such a "fragile majority" Keep the ruling party in Congress. Many important votes. All in trouble. It feels a bit like now. March 1996. After Huige was elected as the first democratically elected president. As Vice President. Lien Chan, who also serves as the Prime Minister. The opposition party strongly resisted. Our brother Hui is worried. Even if a new premier is nominated. It is also difficult to obtain the consent of the Legislative Yuan. Plus we are so sharp. Sharpening the knife to start a career. Taiwan Provincial Governor James Soong. But in the end, it turned out. Elected President and Elected Governor. It is difficult to coexist in the political ecology. After all, where should the two-headed carriage go? Maybe in the end, we are still spinning in circles. So Brother Hui convened a "National Development Conference" Want to make the Kuomintang. and the Democratic Progressive Party. Sit down and discuss cooperation. So what follows is. The fourth constitutional amendment in 1997. Of course, this constitutional amendment. The most well-known. It means "saving" or "freezing" Send the governor and the provincial council into history. Our Song Cup. Only once every four years. Just like the Olympics. I don't know if he still has a chance. Just come out again. Others, like the president. Vice President's impeachment power. Because the Supervisory Commission. No longer a public institution. So it was changed to be proposed by the Legislative Yuan. Finally, there are regulations. The President appoints the Premier. No need for Legislative Yuan approval. Because it was cancelled. Legislative Yuan's right to approve the premier. The president is free to appoint the premier. The position of the Premier has been weakened. "Executive Director of Presidential Policy" But he is still the highest administrative chief. The Legislative Yuan has the power to. Submit a no-confidence motion to overthrow the cabinet. The President of the Executive Yuan can also. Petition the President to dissolve Congress. to check and balance each other. Next is what cannot be ignored. The fifth constitutional amendment in 1999. After all, this is a disgrace to the Constitution of the Republic of China. The provisions concerning the term of office. of representatives of the National Assembly. In the name of "electoral reform" Representatives to the National Assembly from the 4th. The party proportional representation system. The number of people was reduced to 300. This sounds pretty normal. But they want to unify the term of office. So the third National. There is still a fourth term for the Legislative Yuan. All were extended to June 30, 2002. The term of office of the fourth legislator. Extended for 5 months. The term of office of the third National Representative. It was extended for more than 2 years. So everyone said that the national representative was enriching himself. Due to the National Assembly's own extension of the term of office. It sparked strong criticism from the public. As for the fallen area. Changing the constitution to allow yourself to stay in office longer. Does anyone dare to criticize? The Kuomintang also decided under pressure from public opinion. This constitutional amendment. Sent to the Grand Justice of the Judiciary Yuan for interpretation. Last Grand Justice. After the 2000 presidential election. Interpretation No. 499 was made.

The Third National Congress. There are obvious and major flaws. in the constitutional amendment process. The essence of constitutional amendment violates the spirit. of constitutionalism. The constitutional amendment results will become invalid. from the date the interpretation is published. The 2000 election saw the first change of political parties. NUS disarms itself under social pressure. The sixth constitutional amendment. Change the National Assembly representatives. from permanent to task-based. and reduce the powers of the National Assembly. The Legislative Yuan is the prerequisite for initiation. The Legislative Yuan also expanded its powers. Taking the initiative in constitutional amendment. This constitutional amendment clearly shows that the. "Congress" of the Republic of China. The two parliaments will. Moving to a "unitary parliament" system. Brother Hui spent 9 years in office. The constitution has been amended six times. Head of State and Congress. Direct popular election. and legislation to protect citizens' right to vote. Constitutional scholars believe that these constitutional amendments. Sovereignty and independence. Strictly abide by the administration according to law. Pursuit of democracy. The important implications of public supervision. of government administration. It also highlights the separation of powers in democratic countries. The universal concept. of democratic politics with checks and balances. It also allows for "party rotation" where people step down. if they fail to do their job well. It has become the norm in the political arena of the Republic of China. No more past, anti-communism, restoration of the country. Mobilization to suppress rebellion. The shadow of authoritarian rule with unlimited terms. This "bloodless and quiet revolution" Establishing the Republic of China. A national operating system with "efficiency" and "democracy" Next, let’s talk about. The Republic of China's most recent successful constitutional amendment. This is the seventh constitutional amendment. 2005 was the seventh time the. Republic of China amended its constitution. This was also the last successful constitutional amendment. There were many calls from the public at that time. Everyone hopes that the number of seats. in the Legislative Yuan will be halved. Because there are too many seats for legislators. Let politicians who fight or have extreme views. Entering Congress caused political chaos. And after the first change of political parties. The Democratic Progressive Party became the ruling party. The KMT still holds a majority in the Legislative Yuan. Party conflict is on the rise. This further highlights the low efficiency of the meeting. In order to comply with public opinion, the Chen government. In response to calls for congressional reform. In addition, at the end of 2004. Presidential and legislative elections approaching. The ruling Democratic Progressive Party. The largest opposition party, the Kuomintang. A rare consensus. Jointly passed the 7th constitutional amendment. They abolished the mission-based National Assembly. The Legislative Yuan was formally established. A unicameral Congress. Impeachment of the President and Vice President. The proposal must be made by more than half of all legislators. By more than 2/3 of all members. The Constitutional Court. Legislative seats. The number of seats was halved from 225 to 113. Plural constituency system. Single-member district two-vote system. Political parties that received more than 5% of the vote. To be included in the list of elected. The content of this constitutional amendment. Greatly reduced the living space of small parties. People First Party. Opposition from other opposition parties such. as the Taiwan Solidarity Union. However, with the KMT and DPP having an advantage in seats,.

Still all passed. From then on, the political ecology of the Republic of China. Gradually moving towards a two-party system. But there is also a constitutional amendment. It also led to the subsequent. There has been no constitutional amendment for nearly 20 years. That is, the constitutional amendment. There is also a case of territorial change. Must be put to a referendum. According to this constitutional amendment,. Article 12 of the Constitutional Amendment. The constitutional amendment must have. One quarter of the legislators proposed. 3/4 attended. Resolution by 3/4 of the members present. To propose a constitutional amendment. This means that the constitutional amendment bill must be passed. With existing legislators. 113 seats for calculation. At least 29 legislators must propose. 85 legislators attended. Then among the 85 legislators present. 64-bit resolution passed. The constitutional amendment bill will be established. It's really too high. After that, you will have to go through. Our electoral referendum. Valid votes must exceed half of the total number of voters. According to the information from the Central Election Commission. By 2024. The presidential election data. The number of valid voters is approximately 19.94 million. If the representative wants to successfully amend the constitution. More than 9.77 million people must agree. To successfully amend the Constitution. And even our 2020. The most vote-getting presidential. and vice-presidential combination in history. It only received 8.17 million votes. This makes constitutional amendment almost an "impossible task" But at that time, the Kuomintang was thinking. Even if the political parties change. I can't let you DPP. There is a chance to change the constitution. to promote Taiwan independence. The Democratic Progressive Party wants. "Through the high threshold of constitutional amendment. The goal of promoting substantive constitution-making. So, with both parties having their own agenda. This constitutional amendment was passed. Although the constitutional amendment procedures. in various countries around the world. Usually more difficult than amending the law. But if we have a "strict absolute majority" Such a high threshold. Probably even rarer than Japanese compressors. Although the threshold is very high. Irrational majority violence can be prevented. But considering the historical context of the Republic of China. And our opposing issues. In fact, a relatively low referendum threshold can be adopted. After all, during the period of the proposal in the Legislative Yuan. In theory, there will be additional layers of review. There are unlimited discussions. Then let's talk. The most recent constitutional amendment. Although it ended in failure. But we can still see some things. Talking about 2016. The political landscape of Congress has changed again. President Tsai Ing-wen wins the election. In addition, the Democratic Progressive Party holds a majority. in the Legislative Yuan. Achieved full control. The start of the 18-year-old civil rights movement. Breakthrough of the package amendment strategy. In 2021. Successfully lowered the age of majority under the Civil Code. At that time, it was believed. Since the civil and criminal laws have the same meaning. It's been changed to 18. Does that mean that the age for obtaining citizenship must be 18? Therefore, calls for constitutional amendment have re-emerged. Youth NGO groups, mainly the Youth Democratic Association. Also join the 18-year-old citizenship initiative. Promote more high school vocational education. College Student Self-Government Organization.

Children and youth representatives actively participated in promoting. More on March 25, 2022. When the Legislative Yuan was considering the constitutional. amendment on the 18-year-old citizenship. Mobilized more than 100 high school students. Watching the whole process next to the Legislative Yuan. Finally, each political party was asked to hand in 109 votes. A brilliant transcript showing all the passed grades. I rarely saw it in the past. The various political parties in the Legislative Yuan. Such a consensus occurs. So this constitutional amendment. It should go smoothly, right? But Ruifan. You are still too young. Although the constitutional amendment bill has been sent. to the Legislative Yuan. Let everyone vote. But the referendum result was not as expected. The number of voters this time. There are nearly 11 million votes. 5.647 million votes in favor. About 53%. 5.016 million votes against. About 47%. Passing the constitutional amendment threshold. At least 4 million votes are missing. If constitutional amendment is a manifestation of public opinion. The referendum results reflect. Over the past 10 years. Society's attitude towards citizenship at 18. So this topic. Not because of political party support. A breakthrough development. even so. Under the high wall of constitutional amendment. Even if the votes for yes outweigh the votes for no. This is indeed an important progress. The result of this constitutional amendment. It can be said that it is a demonstration. of the constitutional amendment system. It's like "confiscating" the people's right to amend the constitution. Many social movement groups have proposed. We must first solve the problem of lowering the threshold. for constitutional amendment. But the problem will return to the original point. After all, lowering the threshold for constitutional amendment. It still needs to be decided by a referendum. As of now. It seems like an infinite loop bug. There are also some civil society groups that reform the constitution. Decided to take a different approach. Propose matching designs. Citizen Review Mechanism for Constitutional Amendment. Direct Constitution. Long-time constitutional advocate. Convener of the United for Economic Democracy. Lawyer Lai Zhongqiang believes. The most likely way to draft a constitution. The Legislative Yuan adopts a simple majority vote. To pass a new constitution. Then use the method of advocating the exercise. of "constitutional power" Referendum. No need to be subject to a majority of voters. As long as there is a majority of voters. You can pass smoothly. Just like in 2008. Kosovo's Prime Minister reads out the. Kosovo Declaration of Independence. Passed by Parliament. Kosovo's parliament begins drafting new constitution. Kosovo also gained independence that year. The Republic of China has amended its constitution several times. Basically, the Constitution of the Republic of China will not be changed. This structure is followed by. "Additional Articles to the Constitution of the Republic of China" Not moving to the text of the Constitution. No sensitive issues will be encountered. That is "amending the constitution is Taiwan independence" Constitution making is different. It is a thorough formulation of a. Comply with the Constitution of the Republic of China (Taiwan). Taiwan independence. Although the purpose of constitutional amendment. or constitution making. They all hope to have a constitution that suits the current situation.

But the political significance is different. When the National Government retreated to Taiwan. In addition to gold, officers and soldiers. The Constitution of the Republic of China. But this constitution. It was designed for the begonia patch. For example: In the Constitution of the Republic of China. Initially, Taiwan’s indigenous people were not protected. We have no choice but to add articles to the Constitution. of the Republic of China. Add "National affirmation of multiculturalism" Actively maintain and develop. Aboriginal languages ​​and cultures. To protect indigenous peoples. Early 90s. When the constitutional amendment was initiated. Social democratization has just begun. The Kuomintang was the largest party at the time. The "Party-State System" Established. Still leading the country and political development. "Greater China" ideology. Still firmly entrenched in the government system. In Taiwan, which cannot represent "all of China" Amendment to the Constitution. How can we surpass the representative people of China? Where is the text of the Constitution of the Republic of China? So when in 1991. When the constitutional amendment was initiated. The preface of the National Assembly. of the Ten Thousand Years in the "Constitutional Amendments" Plus a premise that is still used today. That is constitutional amendment. "To meet the needs before national unification" Temporary adjustments. "To meet the needs before national unification" The result of this premise is that. Despite seven constitutional amendments. The overall legal framework of the Republic of China government. I can't think of myself as a complete country. Nor can we face the CCP. The fact that there is already a shared. Therefore, there will still be. Court ruling cites Constitution. Claiming that the Republic of China has not given up. its sovereignty over the other side of the strait. And regard the "mainland area" as territory. Crime in the Occupied Areas. Our courts still have. Jurisdiction. The Criminal Law of the Republic of China shall apply. Other court decisions. The Republic of China has undergone seven constitutional amendments. During the first constitutional amendment. The Temporary Provisions during the Period. of Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion were abolished. Normalizing the Constitution. The people also regained the protection of the Constitution. Freedom of speech, freedom of assembly, freedom of religion, etc. Later people. Direct election of the president and vice president. Strengthening protection for indigenous peoples. The National Assembly was finally abolished. Representatives to the National Assembly elected. by the people in the past. Let them choose their president and vice president. They also have the power to amend the constitution. After the people have the right to directly elect the president,. the National Assembly. It loses its necessity to exist. Therefore, the last constitutional amendment. This system has been abolished. From various signs, it can be seen. The current constitutional system of the Republic of China. It is different from the previous Constitution of the Republic of China. And that "is to meet the needs before national unification" What do you think? alright. That's it for this episode. 最近釋憲吵得是轟轟烈烈！

除了死刑要釋憲以外

國會五法也因為其他院會反彈

進入了釋憲程序

但《憲法》做為國家的根本大法

不只規範了政府的權力範圍

同時也保障了人民的各種權利

但法律是需要與時俱進的

沒有一部法律是完美的

也沒有一部法律

可以永遠不跟隨時代修訂

所以很多國家

都有過修憲的紀錄

甚至是重新訂定憲法

所以今天就讓我們來講

中華民國的修憲史吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #taiwan #歷史 #中共 #中國 #修憲 #憲法 #大法官 #法治 #民主 #公民 #法治 #法律 #憲法法庭 #解釋憲法 #history #我的學習筆記 #中華民國憲法 #李登輝 #陳水扁 #宋楚瑜 #七次

Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. There has been a lot of debate over constitutional. interpretation lately. In addition to the death penalty,. The five congressional bills also received backlash from other chambers. Entered the constitutional process. But the Constitution, as the fundamental law of the country,. It not only regulates the scope of government power. It also protects the rights of the people. But the law needs to keep pace with the times. There is no law. You can never revise with the times. So many countries. There is a record of constitutional amendments. Even redefining the constitution. So today let’s talk about. The history of constitutional amendment in the Republic of China. Constitution of the Republic of China. The earliest can be traced back to 1912. That is, the wartime constitution of the first year. of the Republic of China. The Provisional Constitution of the Tutelage Period of 1931. The 1936 Draft Constitution. The constitution was finally drafted in 1946. In fact, we have talked about these. Friends who have forgotten can review it again. The Constitution of the Republic of China in 1947. That is, it was promulgated and implemented in 1947. The full text has 14 chapters in total. 175 articles. The main feature is to highlight the Three Principles of the People. The concept of sovereignty of the people. Clearly define the protection of people’s freedom and rights. Central government system that stipulates the separation. of five powers. There is also a system of local self-government. Directly express the central and local. The division of authority adopts the equal power system. And clearly state the basic national policies, etc. But then you also knew. Our national fortune is so bad. War. In 1948. In response to the Civil War. Subsidiary provisions implementing the Constitution. That is the. "Provisional Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion" And because of the mobilization to suppress the rebellion. Temporary Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion. will take precedence over the Constitution of the Republic of China. Until 1991. Former President Lee Teng-hui conducted. After the first amendment to the Constitution. The Temporary Provisions of Mobilization. for the Suppression of Rebellion were abolished. Since then, there have been seven constitutional amendments. During his 12-year presidency, former President Lee. There have been 6 constitutional amendments in total. The most recent one was in 2005. That is, during the presidency of Chen Shui-bian. So have you discovered. We haven't amended the constitution in nearly 20 years. There is nothing wrong with this constitution. Don’t need to keep up with the times? Or is the threshold for constitutional amendment too high? Is it impossible to achieve? Let's start with former President Lee. After the lifting of martial law in 1987. Politics gradually moved towards democratization. Calls for constitutional reform are beginning to emerge. President Chiang Ching-kuo died the following year. Vice President Lee Teng-hui succeeded. During his 12 years in office. Passed 6 constitutional amendments. Complete the bloodless "Quiet Revolution" Let the Republic of China move away from authoritarianism. Transformed into a democratic country. Brother Hui is the first. Directly through the elected president. Known as "Mr. Democracy" In January 1988. Huigo succeeds as president. Two years later. Old delegates to the National Assembly. I feel like Xiao Jiang is gone. No one can cure us. So I want to expand my power.

But I didn't expect it to cause public dissatisfaction. The Wild Lily Student Movement in March. Require the government to truly implement democratic constitutionalism. Congressional re-election. In fact, Brother Hui has been annoyed with this group. of people for a long time. So he followed the surging public sentiment. Brother Hui convened a "national affairs meeting" outside the system. Harnessing the power of civil society for reform. To fight against the conservative forces in the Kuomintang. Join hands with the Democratic Progressive Party. to promote constitutional amendment. So the first constitutional amendment in 1991. It is against this background that. First, the first senior national representative. Responsible for the first phase. of procedural constitutional amendment. First, abolish the Temporary Provisions on Mobilization. for the Suppression of Rebellion. The Republic of China Free Area. That is, Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu are within the scope. Re-election of the Second Central People's Representatives. And re-elect regularly. Implementing the democratic concept of "sovereignty of the people" Let the Wannian National Era go down in history. So that the second national representative. Can be produced legitimately. Next year, the newly elected second national representative. Conducting the second phase of substantive constitutional reform. Legalize local self-government. Provide legal basis for local autonomy. and popular election of heads of state. Also the former National Congress representatives. The power to elect the president was revoked. The presidential term was changed to 4 years. And there's been a lot of noise lately. Supervisory power of the Supervisory Committee. No longer elected. Appointed by the President. But this also makes our Supervisory Commission. Loss of the conditions for a people's representative body. For more details. You can go and have a look. We previously did a study on. "Should the Supervisory Yuan be abolished?" The Republic of China's unique tripartite system. The Legislative Yuan. National Assembly and Control Yuan. Only the Legislative Yuan. and the bi-parliamentary system of the National Assembly. The personnel approval power originally belonged to the Control Yuan. Delegates to the National Assembly. The most important thing is. The Constitutional Court was established. and to hear cases of unconstitutional dissolution of political parties. That is to say, according to the amended provisions. As long as the purpose or behavior of the political party is. Endangering the Republic of China. Or a liberal democratic constitutional order. The Supreme Court has the power. to dissolve the unconstitutional political party. It sounds really fierce. But this constitutional amendment. It also brought about a major change. in the domestic political situation. The Democratic Progressive Party has successively. In the county and city mayoral elections. The choices are getting more and more beautiful. However, there were serious conflicts within the Kuomintang. Some even left the party and set up a "new party" The third constitutional amendment in July 1994. Because the previous constitutional amendment. Only the right of the National Assembly representatives. to vote for the president was abolished. But it didn't say how to choose the president next time. So this constitutional amendment. Direct election of the president. Written into the revised provisions. And limit the scope of the Premier's countersignature power. The constitutional system of our country has also changed. from "Formal Cabinet System" Transition to a semi-presidential system or a dual-head system. It also clearly states that "mountain compatriots" are included in all legal provisions. Corrected to "Aboriginal" Pay more attention to the rights of indigenous peoples. The Legislative Yuan election at the end of 1995.

The Kuomintang won 85 seats. Only 3 more seats than the majority. Such a "fragile majority" Keep the ruling party in Congress. Many important votes. All in trouble. It feels a bit like now. March 1996. After Huige was elected as the first democratically elected president. As Vice President. Lien Chan, who also serves as the Prime Minister. The opposition party strongly resisted. Our brother Hui is worried. Even if a new premier is nominated. It is also difficult to obtain the consent of the Legislative Yuan. Plus we are so sharp. Sharpening the knife to start a career. Taiwan Provincial Governor James Soong. But in the end, it turned out. Elected President and Elected Governor. It is difficult to coexist in the political ecology. After all, where should the two-headed carriage go? Maybe in the end, we are still spinning in circles. So Brother Hui convened a "National Development Conference" Want to make the Kuomintang. and the Democratic Progressive Party. Sit down and discuss cooperation. So what follows is. The fourth constitutional amendment in 1997. Of course, this constitutional amendment. The most well-known. It means "saving" or "freezing" Send the governor and the provincial council into history. Our Song Cup. Only once every four years. Just like the Olympics. I don't know if he still has a chance. Just come out again. Others, like the president. Vice President's impeachment power. Because the Supervisory Commission. No longer a public institution. So it was changed to be proposed by the Legislative Yuan. Finally, there are regulations. The President appoints the Premier. No need for Legislative Yuan approval. Because it was cancelled. Legislative Yuan's right to approve the premier. The president is free to appoint the premier. The position of the Premier has been weakened. "Executive Director of Presidential Policy" But he is still the highest administrative chief. The Legislative Yuan has the power to. Submit a no-confidence motion to overthrow the cabinet. The President of the Executive Yuan can also. Petition the President to dissolve Congress. to check and balance each other. Next is what cannot be ignored. The fifth constitutional amendment in 1999. After all, this is a disgrace to the Constitution of the Republic of China. The provisions concerning the term of office. of representatives of the National Assembly. In the name of "electoral reform" Representatives to the National Assembly from the 4th. The party proportional representation system. The number of people was reduced to 300. This sounds pretty normal. But they want to unify the term of office. So the third National. There is still a fourth term for the Legislative Yuan. All were extended to June 30, 2002. The term of office of the fourth legislator. Extended for 5 months. The term of office of the third National Representative. It was extended for more than 2 years. So everyone said that the national representative was enriching himself. Due to the National Assembly's own extension of the term of office. It sparked strong criticism from the public. As for the fallen area. Changing the constitution to allow yourself to stay in office longer. Does anyone dare to criticize? The Kuomintang also decided under pressure from public opinion. This constitutional amendment. Sent to the Grand Justice of the Judiciary Yuan for interpretation. Last Grand Justice. After the 2000 presidential election. Interpretation No. 499 was made.

The Third National Congress. There are obvious and major flaws. in the constitutional amendment process. The essence of constitutional amendment violates the spirit. of constitutionalism. The constitutional amendment results will become invalid. from the date the interpretation is published. The 2000 election saw the first change of political parties. NUS disarms itself under social pressure. The sixth constitutional amendment. Change the National Assembly representatives. from permanent to task-based. and reduce the powers of the National Assembly. The Legislative Yuan is the prerequisite for initiation. The Legislative Yuan also expanded its powers. Taking the initiative in constitutional amendment. This constitutional amendment clearly shows that the. "Congress" of the Republic of China. The two parliaments will. Moving to a "unitary parliament" system. Brother Hui spent 9 years in office. The constitution has been amended six times. Head of State and Congress. Direct popular election. and legislation to protect citizens' right to vote. Constitutional scholars believe that these constitutional amendments. Sovereignty and independence. Strictly abide by the administration according to law. Pursuit of democracy. The important implications of public supervision. of government administration. It also highlights the separation of powers in democratic countries. The universal concept. of democratic politics with checks and balances. It also allows for "party rotation" where people step down. if they fail to do their job well. It has become the norm in the political arena of the Republic of China. No more past, anti-communism, restoration of the country. Mobilization to suppress rebellion. The shadow of authoritarian rule with unlimited terms. This "bloodless and quiet revolution" Establishing the Republic of China. A national operating system with "efficiency" and "democracy" Next, let’s talk about. The Republic of China's most recent successful constitutional amendment. This is the seventh constitutional amendment. 2005 was the seventh time the. Republic of China amended its constitution. This was also the last successful constitutional amendment. There were many calls from the public at that time. Everyone hopes that the number of seats. in the Legislative Yuan will be halved. Because there are too many seats for legislators. Let politicians who fight or have extreme views. Entering Congress caused political chaos. And after the first change of political parties. The Democratic Progressive Party became the ruling party. The KMT still holds a majority in the Legislative Yuan. Party conflict is on the rise. This further highlights the low efficiency of the meeting. In order to comply with public opinion, the Chen government. In response to calls for congressional reform. In addition, at the end of 2004. Presidential and legislative elections approaching. The ruling Democratic Progressive Party. The largest opposition party, the Kuomintang. A rare consensus. Jointly passed the 7th constitutional amendment. They abolished the mission-based National Assembly. The Legislative Yuan was formally established. A unicameral Congress. Impeachment of the President and Vice President. The proposal must be made by more than half of all legislators. By more than 2/3 of all members. The Constitutional Court. Legislative seats. The number of seats was halved from 225 to 113. Plural constituency system. Single-member district two-vote system. Political parties that received more than 5% of the vote. To be included in the list of elected. The content of this constitutional amendment. Greatly reduced the living space of small parties. People First Party. Opposition from other opposition parties such. as the Taiwan Solidarity Union. However, with the KMT and DPP having an advantage in seats,.

Still all passed. From then on, the political ecology of the Republic of China. Gradually moving towards a two-party system. But there is also a constitutional amendment. It also led to the subsequent. There has been no constitutional amendment for nearly 20 years. That is, the constitutional amendment. There is also a case of territorial change. Must be put to a referendum. According to this constitutional amendment,. Article 12 of the Constitutional Amendment. The constitutional amendment must have. One quarter of the legislators proposed. 3/4 attended. Resolution by 3/4 of the members present. To propose a constitutional amendment. This means that the constitutional amendment bill must be passed. With existing legislators. 113 seats for calculation. At least 29 legislators must propose. 85 legislators attended. Then among the 85 legislators present. 64-bit resolution passed. The constitutional amendment bill will be established. It's really too high. After that, you will have to go through. Our electoral referendum. Valid votes must exceed half of the total number of voters. According to the information from the Central Election Commission. By 2024. The presidential election data. The number of valid voters is approximately 19.94 million. If the representative wants to successfully amend the constitution. More than 9.77 million people must agree. To successfully amend the Constitution. And even our 2020. The most vote-getting presidential. and vice-presidential combination in history. It only received 8.17 million votes. This makes constitutional amendment almost an "impossible task" But at that time, the Kuomintang was thinking. Even if the political parties change. I can't let you DPP. There is a chance to change the constitution. to promote Taiwan independence. The Democratic Progressive Party wants. "Through the high threshold of constitutional amendment. The goal of promoting substantive constitution-making. So, with both parties having their own agenda. This constitutional amendment was passed. Although the constitutional amendment procedures. in various countries around the world. Usually more difficult than amending the law. But if we have a "strict absolute majority" Such a high threshold. Probably even rarer than Japanese compressors. Although the threshold is very high. Irrational majority violence can be prevented. But considering the historical context of the Republic of China. And our opposing issues. In fact, a relatively low referendum threshold can be adopted. After all, during the period of the proposal in the Legislative Yuan. In theory, there will be additional layers of review. There are unlimited discussions. Then let's talk. The most recent constitutional amendment. Although it ended in failure. But we can still see some things. Talking about 2016. The political landscape of Congress has changed again. President Tsai Ing-wen wins the election. In addition, the Democratic Progressive Party holds a majority. in the Legislative Yuan. Achieved full control. The start of the 18-year-old civil rights movement. Breakthrough of the package amendment strategy. In 2021. Successfully lowered the age of majority under the Civil Code. At that time, it was believed. Since the civil and criminal laws have the same meaning. It's been changed to 18. Does that mean that the age for obtaining citizenship must be 18? Therefore, calls for constitutional amendment have re-emerged. Youth NGO groups, mainly the Youth Democratic Association. Also join the 18-year-old citizenship initiative. Promote more high school vocational education. College Student Self-Government Organization.

Children and youth representatives actively participated in promoting. More on March 25, 2022. When the Legislative Yuan was considering the constitutional. amendment on the 18-year-old citizenship. Mobilized more than 100 high school students. Watching the whole process next to the Legislative Yuan. Finally, each political party was asked to hand in 109 votes. A brilliant transcript showing all the passed grades. I rarely saw it in the past. The various political parties in the Legislative Yuan. Such a consensus occurs. So this constitutional amendment. It should go smoothly, right? But Ruifan. You are still too young. Although the constitutional amendment bill has been sent. to the Legislative Yuan. Let everyone vote. But the referendum result was not as expected. The number of voters this time. There are nearly 11 million votes. 5.647 million votes in favor. About 53%. 5.016 million votes against. About 47%. Passing the constitutional amendment threshold. At least 4 million votes are missing. If constitutional amendment is a manifestation of public opinion. The referendum results reflect. Over the past 10 years. Society's attitude towards citizenship at 18. So this topic. Not because of political party support. A breakthrough development. even so. Under the high wall of constitutional amendment. Even if the votes for yes outweigh the votes for no. This is indeed an important progress. The result of this constitutional amendment. It can be said that it is a demonstration. of the constitutional amendment system. It's like "confiscating" the people's right to amend the constitution. Many social movement groups have proposed. We must first solve the problem of lowering the threshold. for constitutional amendment. But the problem will return to the original point. After all, lowering the threshold for constitutional amendment. It still needs to be decided by a referendum. As of now. It seems like an infinite loop bug. There are also some civil society groups that reform the constitution. Decided to take a different approach. Propose matching designs. Citizen Review Mechanism for Constitutional Amendment. Direct Constitution. Long-time constitutional advocate. Convener of the United for Economic Democracy. Lawyer Lai Zhongqiang believes. The most likely way to draft a constitution. The Legislative Yuan adopts a simple majority vote. To pass a new constitution. Then use the method of advocating the exercise. of "constitutional power" Referendum. No need to be subject to a majority of voters. As long as there is a majority of voters. You can pass smoothly. Just like in 2008. Kosovo's Prime Minister reads out the. Kosovo Declaration of Independence. Passed by Parliament. Kosovo's parliament begins drafting new constitution. Kosovo also gained independence that year. The Republic of China has amended its constitution several times. Basically, the Constitution of the Republic of China will not be changed. This structure is followed by. "Additional Articles to the Constitution of the Republic of China" Not moving to the text of the Constitution. No sensitive issues will be encountered. That is "amending the constitution is Taiwan independence" Constitution making is different. It is a thorough formulation of a. Comply with the Constitution of the Republic of China (Taiwan). Taiwan independence. Although the purpose of constitutional amendment. or constitution making. They all hope to have a constitution that suits the current situation.

But the political significance is different. When the National Government retreated to Taiwan. In addition to gold, officers and soldiers. The Constitution of the Republic of China. But this constitution. It was designed for the begonia patch. For example: In the Constitution of the Republic of China. Initially, Taiwan’s indigenous people were not protected. We have no choice but to add articles to the Constitution. of the Republic of China. Add "National affirmation of multiculturalism" Actively maintain and develop. Aboriginal languages ​​and cultures. To protect indigenous peoples. Early 90s. When the constitutional amendment was initiated. Social democratization has just begun. The Kuomintang was the largest party at the time. The "Party-State System" Established. Still leading the country and political development. "Greater China" ideology. Still firmly entrenched in the government system. In Taiwan, which cannot represent "all of China" Amendment to the Constitution. How can we surpass the representative people of China? Where is the text of the Constitution of the Republic of China? So when in 1991. When the constitutional amendment was initiated. The preface of the National Assembly. of the Ten Thousand Years in the "Constitutional Amendments" Plus a premise that is still used today. That is constitutional amendment. "To meet the needs before national unification" Temporary adjustments. "To meet the needs before national unification" The result of this premise is that. Despite seven constitutional amendments. The overall legal framework of the Republic of China government. I can't think of myself as a complete country. Nor can we face the CCP. The fact that there is already a shared. Therefore, there will still be. Court ruling cites Constitution. Claiming that the Republic of China has not given up. its sovereignty over the other side of the strait. And regard the "mainland area" as territory. Crime in the Occupied Areas. Our courts still have. Jurisdiction. The Criminal Law of the Republic of China shall apply. Other court decisions. The Republic of China has undergone seven constitutional amendments. During the first constitutional amendment. The Temporary Provisions during the Period. of Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion were abolished. Normalizing the Constitution. The people also regained the protection of the Constitution. Freedom of speech, freedom of assembly, freedom of religion, etc. Later people. Direct election of the president and vice president. Strengthening protection for indigenous peoples. The National Assembly was finally abolished. Representatives to the National Assembly elected. by the people in the past. Let them choose their president and vice president. They also have the power to amend the constitution. After the people have the right to directly elect the president,. the National Assembly. It loses its necessity to exist. Therefore, the last constitutional amendment. This system has been abolished. From various signs, it can be seen. The current constitutional system of the Republic of China. It is different from the previous Constitution of the Republic of China. And that "is to meet the needs before national unification" What do you think? alright. That's it for this episode. 最近釋憲吵得是轟轟烈烈！

除了死刑要釋憲以外

國會五法也因為其他院會反彈

進入了釋憲程序

但《憲法》做為國家的根本大法

不只規範了政府的權力範圍

同時也保障了人民的各種權利

但法律是需要與時俱進的

沒有一部法律是完美的

也沒有一部法律

可以永遠不跟隨時代修訂

所以很多國家

都有過修憲的紀錄

甚至是重新訂定憲法

所以今天就讓我們來講

中華民國的修憲史吧！

#臺灣 #台灣 #taiwan #歷史 #中共 #中國 #修憲 #憲法 #大法官 #法治 #民主 #公民 #法治 #法律 #憲法法庭 #解釋憲法 #history #我的學習筆記 #中華民國憲法 #李登輝 #陳水扁 #宋楚瑜 #七次

。《公視新聞網》https://news.pts.org.tw/article/603052

Hello! Hello everyone! I am Brother Niu. There has been a lot of debate over constitutional. interpretation lately. In addition to the death penalty,. The five congressional bills also received backlash from other chambers. Entered the constitutional process. But the Constitution, as the fundamental law of the country,. It not only regulates the scope of government power. It also protects the rights of the people. But the law needs to keep pace with the times. There is no law. You can never revise with the times. So many countries. There is a record of constitutional amendments. Even redefining the constitution. So today let’s talk about. The history of constitutional amendment in the Republic of China. Constitution of the Republic of China. The earliest can be traced back to 1912. That is, the wartime constitution of the first year. of the Republic of China. The Provisional Constitution of the Tutelage Period of 1931. The 1936 Draft Constitution. The constitution was finally drafted in 1946. In fact, we have talked about these. Friends who have forgotten can review it again. The Constitution of the Republic of China in 1947. That is, it was promulgated and implemented in 1947. The full text has 14 chapters in total. 175 articles. The main feature is to highlight the Three Principles of the People. The concept of sovereignty of the people. Clearly define the protection of people’s freedom and rights. Central government system that stipulates the separation. of five powers. There is also a system of local self-government. Directly express the central and local. The division of authority adopts the equal power system. And clearly state the basic national policies, etc. But then you also knew. Our national fortune is so bad. War. In 1948. In response to the Civil War. Subsidiary provisions implementing the Constitution. That is the. "Provisional Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion" And because of the mobilization to suppress the rebellion. Temporary Provisions on Mobilization for the Suppression of Rebellion. will take precedence over the Constitution of the Republic of China. Until 1991. Former President Lee Teng-hui conducted. After the first amendment to the Constitution. The Temporary Provisions of Mobilization. for the Suppression of Rebellion were abolished. Since then, there have been seven constitutional amendments. During his 12-year presidency, former President Lee. There have been 6 constitutional amendments in total. The most recent one was in 2005. That is, during the presidency of Chen Shui-bian. So have you discovered. We haven't amended the constitution in nearly 20 years. There is nothing wrong with this constitution. Don’t need to keep up with the times? Or is the threshold for constitutional amendment too high? Is it impossible to achieve? Let's start with former President Lee. After the lifting of martial law in 1987. Politics gradually moved towards democratization. Calls for constitutional reform are beginning to emerge. President Chiang Ching-kuo died the following year. Vice President Lee Teng-hui succeeded. During his 12 years in office. Passed 6 constitutional amendments. Complete the bloodless "Quiet Revolution" Let the Republic of China move away from authoritarianism. 最近釋憲吵得是轟轟烈烈！

除了死刑要釋憲以外

國會五法也因為其他院會反彈

進入了釋憲程序

但《憲法》做為國家的根本大法

不只規範了政府的權力範圍

同時也保障了人民的各種權利

但法律是需要與時俱進的

沒有一部法律是完美的

也沒有一部法律

可以永遠不跟隨時代修訂

所以很多國家

都有過修憲的紀錄

甚至是重新訂定憲法

所以今天就讓我們來講

中華民國的修憲史吧！
